656→    One \"turn\" = one Claude subprocess invocation (including retries).\n   657→    can_launch() must return False when remaining turns are insufficient,\n   658→    triggering a HALTED outcome path.\n   692→\n   693→    Algorithm:\n   694→      1. Take directory name (not full path).\n   695→      2. Strip leading 'sc-' prefix (case-insensitive).\n   696→      3. Strip trailing '-protocol' suffix (case-insensitive).\n   697→      4. If result is empty, raise DerivationFailedError.\n   699→    \"\"\"\n   700→    name = workflow_path.name if workflow_path.is_file() else workflow_path.name\n   701→    # Normalize to lowercase\n   702→    name = name.lower()\n   703→    # Strip sc- prefix\n   704→    if name.startswith(\"sc-\"):\n   705→        name = name[3:]\n   706→    # Strip -protocol suffix\n   707→    if name.endswith(\"-protocol\"):\n   708→        name = name[: -len(\"-protocol\")]\n   709→    # Normalize: keep only lowercase letters, digits, hyphens\n   710→    import re\n   711→    name = re.sub(r\"[^a-z0-9-]\", \"-\", name)\n   712→    # Collapse multiple hyphens\n   713→    name = re.sub(r\"-+\", \"-\", name).strip(\"-\")\n   714→\n   715→    if not name:\n   716→        raise DerivationFailedError(workflow_path.name)\n   717→    return name\n   718→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. line_count=agent.line_count,\n                    purpose=\"agent definition\",\n                )\n            )\n\n        return ComponentInventory(\n            components=components,\n            source_skill=source_skill,\n            source_command=source_command,\n        )\n\n    def to_manifest_markdown(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Render a Markdown manifest with YAML frontmatter.\"\"\"\n        source_command = self.command.name if self.command else \"\"\n        source_skill = self.skill.name if self.skill else \"\"\n\n        lines = [\n            \"---\",\n          \"---\",\n            \"\",\n        ]\n\n        if self.component_count == 0:\n            lines.append(\"No components discovered.\")\n            return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n        if           ]\n\n        if self.skill is not None:\n            lines += [\n                \"## Skill (Tier 1)\",\n                f\"- **{self.skill.name}** ({self.skill.line_count} lines)\",\n                \"\",\n            ]\n\n        if self.agents:\n            lines += [\"## Agents (Tier 2)\"]\n            for agent in self.agents:\n                status = \"found\" if agent.found else at least one turn remains.\"\"\"\n        return self.remaining > 0\n\n    def reset(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Reset consumed turns (for testing only).\"\"\"\n        self._consumed = 0\n\n\ndef If result is empty, raise DerivationFailedError.\n    \"\"\"\n    name = workflow_path.name if workflow_path.is_file() else workflow_path.name\n    # Normalize to lowercase\n    name = name.lower()\n    # Strip sc- prefix\n    if name.startswith(\"sc-\"):\n        name = name[3:]\n    # Strip -protocol suffix\n    if name.endswith(\"-protocol\"):\n        name = name[: -len(\"-protocol\")]\n    # Normalize: keep only lowercase letters, digits, hyphens\n    import re\n    name = re.sub(r\"[^a-z0-9-]\", \"-\", name)\n    # Collapse multiple hyphens\n    name = re.sub(r\"-+\", \"-\", name).strip(\"-\")\n\n    if not name:\n        raise DerivationFailedError(workflow_path.name)\n    return name\n","numLines":718,"startLine":1,"totalLines":718}}}

iterations reached or budget/user escalation.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        max_iterations: int = 3,\n        budget_guard: Optional[SimpleBudgetGuard] = None,\n    ) -> = 0\n        self._escalation_reason: Optional[EscalationReason] = None\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Public properties\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @property\n    def state(self) -> ConvergenceState:\n        return self._state\n\n    @property\n    def is_done(self) -> bool:\n        return self._state != ConvergenceState.RUNNING\n\n    @property\n    def current_iteration(self) -> int:\n        return self._current_iteration\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Core API\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def submit(self, iteration_result: IterationResult) -> ConvergenceState:\n        \"\"\"Submit the result of one iteration and advance the state machine.\n\n        Args:\n            iteration_result: Populated IterationResult for this iteration.\n\n        Returns:\n            The new ConvergenceState after evaluating the result.\n\n        Raises:\n            RuntimeError: If called when the engine is already in a terminal state.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.is_done:\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                f\"Cannot submit to engine in terminal state {self._state.value}\"\n            )\n\n        self._current_iteration = iteration_result.iteration\n\n        # CONVERGED: zero unaddressed CRITICALs\n        if iteration_result.unaddressed_criticals == 0:\n            self._state = ConvergenceState.CONVERGED\n            return self._state\n\n        # ESCALATED: max iterations exhausted\n        if self._current_iteration >= self._max_iterations:\n            self._state = ConvergenceState.ESCALATED\n            self._escalation_reason = EscalationReason.MAX_ITERATIONS\n            return self._state\n\n        # Continue running\n        return self._state\n\n    def check_budget(self, required: float) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return True if budget guard allows the given spend (or if no guard).\"\"\"\n        if self._budget_guard is None:\n            return True\n        return self._budget_guard.has_budget(required)\n\n    def escalate_budget(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Force ESCALATED state due to budget exhaustion.\"\"\"\n        self._state = ConvergenceState.ESCALATED\n        self._escalation_reason = EscalationReason.BUDGET_EXHAUSTED\n\n    def escalate_user(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Force ESCALATED state due to user rejection.\"\"\"\n        self._state = ConvergenceState.ESCALATED\n        self._escalation_reason = EscalationReason.USER_REJECTED\n\n    def result(self) -> ConvergenceResult:\n        \"\"\"Return the final convergence result.\n\n        Should be called after the engine is done (is_done == True).\n        \"\"\"\n        return ConvergenceResult(\n            state=self._state,\n            iterations_completed=self._current_iteration,\n            escalation_reason=self._escalation_reason,\n        )\n","numLines":240,"startLine":1,"totalLines":240}}}

RESUMABILITY_MATRIX.get(step_name)\n    if entry is not None:\n        for artifact in entry.required_artifacts:\n            p = workdir / artifact\n            if p.exists() and str(p) not in preserved:\n                preserved.append(str(p))\n\n    return StepResumeContext(\n        failed_step=step_name,\n        failed_step_number=step_number,\n        last_completed_step=last_name,\n specific placeholder names.\n\nThis step is RESUMABLE (steps >= 5).\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport re\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nfrom ..models import (\n    FailureClassification,\n    PortifyConfig,\n    PortifyStatus,\n    PortifyStepResult,\n)\nfrom ..process import PortifyProcess, ProcessResult\n\nlog = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\nSTEP_NAME = \"synthesize-spec\"\nSTEP_NUMBER = 5\nPHASE = 3\nGATE_TIER = \"STRICT\"\nARTIFACT_NAME = \"synthesized-spec.md\"\n\nSENTINEL_PATTERN = portify-analysis.md and portify-spec.md from prior steps.\n    Produces synthesized-spec.md with zero sentinels (SC-005).\n    \"\"\"\n    start = time.monotonic()\n    results_dir = config.output_dir / \"results\" if config.output_dir else Path(\"results\")\n    results_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    artifact_path = results_dir / ARTIFACT_NAME\n\n    # Check prerequisites\n    for              step_number=STEP_NUMBER,\n                phase=PHASE,\n                portify_status=PortifyStatus.FAIL,\n duration_seconds=time.monotonic() - start,\n            )\n\n    # Run Claude subprocess\n    process = PortifyProcess(\n        step_name=STEP_NAME,\n        output_file=artifact_path,\n out\",\n            duration_seconds=duration,\n        )\n\n    if result.exit_code != 0:\n        return PortifyStepResult(\n            step_name=STEP_NAME,\n            step_number=STEP_NUMBER,\n            phase=PHASE,\n            portify_status=PortifyStatus.FAIL,\n            failure_classification=FailureClassification.MISSING_ARTIFACT,\n            gate_tier=GATE_TIER,\n            error_message=f\"Subprocess exited {result.exit_code}\",\n            duration_seconds=duration,\n        )\n\n    # Read output content\n    if result.output_file and result.output_file.exists():\n        content = result.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n    elif artifact_path.exists():\n        content = artifact_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n    else:\n        content = result.stdout_text\n\n    # Scan for sentinels (SC-005)\n    sentinels = scan_sentinels(content)\n    if sentinels:\n        return PortifyStepResult(\n            step_name=STEP_NAME,\n            step_number=STEP_NUMBER,\n            phase=PHASE,\n            portify_status=PortifyStatus.FAIL,\n PATH.\"\"\"\n    return shutil.which(\"superclaude\") is not None\n\n\ndef parse_findings(content: str) -> list[GapFinding]:\n    \"\"\"Parse structured gap findings from markdown table output.\n\n    Looks for rows in format: | GAP-NNN | description | severity | section |\n    \"\"\"\n    findings = []\n    pattern = re.compile(\n        r\"\\|\\s*(GAP-\\d+)\\s*\\|\\s*([^|]+)\\s*\\|\\s*(CRITICAL|MAJOR|MINOR|INFO)\\s*\\|\\s*([^|]*)\\s*\\|\",\n        re.MULTILINE,\n    )\n    for m in pattern.finditer(content):\n        findings.append(GapFinding(\n            gap_id=m.group(1).strip(),\n            description=m.group(2).strip(),\n            severity=m.group(3).strip(),\n            affected_section=m.group(4).strip(),\n        ))\n    return findings\n\n\ndef has_section_12_content(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Return True if content contains Section 12 (Gaps and Risks) gate_tier=GATE_TIER,\n            error_message=\"Missing prerequisite: synthesized-spec.md\",\n            duration_seconds=time.monotonic() - start,\n        )\n\n    # Run Claude subprocess\n    process = PortifyProcess(\n        step_name=STEP_NAME,\n        output_file=artifact_path,\n        config=config,\n    )\n    result: ProcessResult = process.run(\n        prompt=_build_prompt(config, spec_path),\n    )\n\n    duration = time.monotonic() - start\n\n    # Handle subprocess failures\n    if result.timed_out or result.exit_code == 124:\n        return PortifyStepResult(\n            step_name=STEP_NAME,\n            step_number=STEP_NUMBER,\n            phase=PHASE,\n            portify_status=PortifyStatus.TIMEOUT,\n            failure_classification=FailureClassification.TIMEOUT,\n            gate_tier=GATE_TIER,\n            error_message=\"Claude subprocess timed out\",\n            duration_seconds=duration,\n        )\n\n    if result.exit_code != 0:\n        return PortifyStepResult(\n            step_name=STEP_NAME,\n            step_number=STEP_NUMBER,\n            phase=PHASE,\n            portify_status=PortifyStatus.FAIL,\n            failure_classification=FailureClassification.MISSING_ARTIFACT,\n            gate_tier=GATE_TIER,\n            error_message=f\"Subprocess exited ConvergenceEngine:\n    \"\"\"State machine for the panel-review convergence loop (FR-030–FR-033).\n\n    Usage::\n\n        engine = ConvergenceEngine(max_iterations=3)\n        for i in range(1, engine.result()\n\n    States:\n        RUNNING  → initial state; iterations may be submitted.\n        CONVERGED → terminal; zero unaddressed CRITICALs achieved.\n        ESCALATED → terminal; max iterations reached or budget/user escalation.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        max_iterations: int = 3,\n        budget_guard: Optional[SimpleBudgetGuard] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._max_iterations = max_iterations\n        self._budget_guard = budget_guard\n        self._state: ConvergenceState = ConvergenceState.RUNNING\n        self._current_iteration: int = 0\n        self._escalation_reason: Optional[EscalationReason] = None\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Public properties\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @property\n    def state(self) -> ConvergenceState:\n        return self._state\n\n    @property\n    def is_done(self) -> bool:\n        return self._state != ConvergenceState.RUNNING\n\n    @property\n    def current_iteration(self) -> int:\n        return self._current_iteration\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Core API\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def submit(self, iteration_result: IterationResult) -> ConvergenceState:\n        \"\"\"Submit the result of one iteration and advance the state machine.\n\n        Args:\n            iteration_result: Populated IterationResult for this iteration.\n\n        Returns:\n            The new ConvergenceState after evaluating the result.\n\n        Raises:\n            RuntimeError: If called when the engine is already in a terminal state.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.is_done:\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                f\"Cannot submit to engine in terminal state {self._state.value}\"\n            )\n\n        self._current_iteration = iteration_result.iteration\n\n        # CONVERGED: zero unaddressed CRITICALs\n        if iteration_result.unaddressed_criticals == 0:\n            self._state = ConvergenceState.CONVERGED\n            return self._state\n\n        # ESCALATED: max iterations exhausted\n        if self._current_iteration >= self._max_iterations:\n            self._state = rejection.\"\"\"\n        self._state = ConvergenceState.ESCALATED\n        self._escalation_reason = EscalationReason.USER_REJECTED\n\n    def result(self) -> ConvergenceResult:\n        \"\"\"Return invocation (including retries).\n    can_launch() must return False when remaining turns are insufficient,\n    triggering a HALTED outcome (FR-040).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, total_budget: int) -> None:\n        self._total_budget: int = total_budget\n        self._consumed: int = 0\n\n    @property\n    def total_budget(self) -> int:\n        return self._total_budget\n\n    @property\n    def consumed(self) -> int:\n        return self._consumed\n\n    @property\n    def remaining(self) -> int:\n        return max(0, self._total_budget - self._consumed)\n\n    def consume(self, turns: int = 1) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Record that `turns` turns were consumed.\"\"\"\n        self._consumed += turns\n\n    def can_launch(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return True if at least one turn remains.\"\"\"\n        return self.remaining > 0\n\n    def reset(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Reset consumed turns (for testing only).\"\"\"\n        self._consumed = 0\n\n\ndef _derive_name_from_path(workflow_path: Path) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive CLI module name from a workflow directory path.\n\n    Algorithm:\n      1. Take directory name (not full path).\n      2. Strip leading 'sc-' prefix (case-insensitive).\n      3. Strip trailing '-protocol' suffix (case-insensitive).\n      4. Normalize to lowercase kebab-case.\n      5. If result is empty, raise DerivationFailedError.\n    \"\"\"\n    name = workflow_path.name if workflow_path.is_file() else workflow_path.name\n    # Normalize to lowercase\n    name = name.lower()\n    # Strip sc- prefix\n    if name.startswith(\"sc-\"):\n        name = name[3:]\n    # Strip -protocol suffix\n    if name.endswith(\"-protocol\"):\n        name = name[: -len(\"-protocol\")]\n    # Normalize: keep only lowercase letters, digits, hyphens\n    import re\n    name = re.sub(r\"[^a-z0-9-]\", \"-\", name)\n    # Collapse multiple hyphens\n    name = re.sub(r\"-+\", \"-\", name).strip(\"-\")\n\n    if not name:\n        raise DerivationFailedError(workflow_path.name)\n    return name\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":483,"oldLines":6,"newStart":483,"newLines":7,"lines":["     error_message: str = \"\"","     duration_seconds: float = 0.0","     review_accepted: Optional[bool] = None","+    review_required: bool = False"," "," "," # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

duration = time.monotonic() - start\n\n    # Handle subprocess failures\n    if result.timed_out or result.exit_code == 124:\n        return PortifyStepResult(\n            step_name=STEP_NAME,\n            step_number=STEP_NUMBER,\n            phase=PHASE,\n            portify_status=PortifyStatus.TIMEOUT,\n            failure_classification=FailureClassification.TIMEOUT,\n            gate_tier=GATE_TIER,\n            error_message=\"Claude subprocess timed out\",\n            duration_seconds=duration,\n        )\n\n    if result.exit_code != 0:\n        return PortifyStepResult(\n            step_name=STEP_NAME,\n            step_number=STEP_NUMBER,\n            phase=PHASE,\n            portify_status=PortifyStatus.FAIL,\n            failure_classification=FailureClassification.MISSING_ARTIFACT,\n            gate_tier=GATE_TIER,\n            error_message=f\"Subprocess exited {result.exit_code}\",\n            duration_seconds=duration,\n        )\n\n    # Read output\n    if result.output_file and result.output_file.exists():\n        content = result.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n    elif artifact_path.exists():\n        content = artifact_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n    else:\n        content = result.stdout_text\n\n    # Gate check (SC-004)\n    if not _check_gate(content):\n        return PortifyStepResult(\n            step_name=STEP_NAME,\n            step_number=STEP_NUMBER,\n            phase=PHASE,\n            portify_status=PortifyStatus.FAIL,\n            failure_classification=FailureClassification.GATE_FAILURE,\n            gate_tier=GATE_TIER,\n            artifact_path=str(artifact_path),\n            error_message=\"Gate SC-004 failed\",\n            duration_seconds=duration,\n        )\n\n    # Write artifact if not already written\n    if not artifact_path.exists():\n        artifact_path.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n    return PortifyStepResult(\n        step_name=STEP_NAME,\n        step_number=STEP_NUMBER,\n        phase=PHASE,\n        portify_status=PortifyStatus.PASS,\n        gate_tier=GATE_TIER,\n        artifact_path=str(artifact_path),\n        duration_seconds=duration,\n    )\n\n\ndef _check_gate(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"SC-004: pipeline_steps frontmatter field must be present and > 0.\"\"\"\n    from ..utils import parse_frontmatter\n    frontmatter, _ = parse_frontmatter(content)\n    return bool(frontmatter) and int(frontmatter.get(\"pipeline_steps\", 0)) > 0\n\n\ndef _build_prompt(config: PortifyConfig, analysis_path: Path) -> str:\n    return (\n        f\"Design a CLI pipeline for the workflow at {config.workflow_path}. \"\n        f\"Analysis: {analysis_path}. \"\n        \"Produce portify-pipeline-design.md with step definitions and gate assignments.\"\n    )\n"}}

Collision detection (NAME_COLLISION, OUTPUT_NOT_WRITABLE)\n  T02.04 — Workdir creation and portify-config.yaml emission\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport os\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional, Union\n\nfrom .models import (\n    AMBIGUOUS_PATH,\n    DERIVATION_FAILED,\n    INVALID_PATH,\n    NAME_COLLISION,\n    OUTPUT_NOT_WRITABLE,\n    PortifyConfig,\n    _derive_name_from_path,\n)\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Known CLI modules that block collision by default (reserved names)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n_KNOWN_CLI_MODULES = frozenset(\n    {\n        \"sprint\",\n        \"roadmap\",\n        \"tasklist\",\n        \"cleanup-audit\",\n        \"cleanup_audit\",\n        \"audit\",\n        \"pipeline\",\n        \"main\",\n    }\n)\n\n\ndef load_portify_config(\n    workflow_path: Union[str, Path],\n    output_dir: Optional[Union[str, Path]] = None,\n    cli_name: Optional[str] = None,\n    commands_dir: Optional[Union[str, Path]] = None,\n    skills_dir: Optional[Union[str, Path]] = None,\n    agents_dir: Optional[Union[str, Path]] = None,\n    include_agents: Optional[Union[bool, list[str]]] = None,\n    save_manifest_path: Optional[Union[str, Path]] = None,\n    dry_run: bool = False,\n    debug: bool = False,\n    max_turns: int = 200,\n    model: str = \"\",\n    stall_timeout: int = 300,\n    skip_review: bool = False,\n) -> PortifyConfig:\n    \"\"\"Construct a PortifyConfig from CLI arguments.\n\n    Does NOT validate — call validate_portify_config() separately.\n\n    Args:\n        workflow_path: Path to the workflow skill directory or SKILL.md file.\n        output_dir: Where generated CLI module will be written. Defaults to\n                    src/superclaude/cli/ relative to workflow_path project root.\n        cli_name: Explicit CLI module name override. If omitted, derived\n                  automatically from workflow directory name.\n        commands_dir: Override for commands discovery directory.\n        skills_dir: Override for skills discovery directory.\n        agents_dir: Override for agents discovery directory.\n        include_agents: If a list, enables agent inclusion and stores the list.\n                        If True/False, sets include_agents flag only.\n        save_manifest_path: Path to write the component manifest.\n        dry_run: Limit run to config validation only.\n        debug: Enable debug logging.\n        max_turns: Maximum subprocess turns allowed.\n        model: Claude model identifier.\n        stall_timeout: Seconds before stall detection triggers.\n    \"\"\"\n    wp = Path(workflow_path).resolve()\n    target_input = str(workflow_path)\n\n    # Resolve optional directories\n    resolved_commands = Path(commands_dir).resolve() if commands_dir else None\n    resolved_skills = Path(skills_dir).resolve() if skills_dir else None\n    resolved_agents = Path(agents_dir).resolve() if agents_dir else None\n    resolved_manifest = Path(save_manifest_path).resolve() if save_manifest_path else None\n    resolved_output = Path(output_dir).resolve() if output_dir else None\n\n    # Handle include_agents\n    include_agents_flag = False\n    include_agents_list: list[str] = []\n    if isinstance(include_agents, list):\n        include_agents_flag = True\n        include_agents_list = include_agents\n    elif include_agents is True:\n        include_agents_flag = True\n\n    config = PortifyConfig(\n        workflow_path=wp,\n        cli_name=cli_name or \"\",\n        output_dir=resolved_output,\n        commands_dir=resolved_commands,\n        skills_dir=resolved_skills,\n        agents_dir=resolved_agents,\n        include_agents=include_agents_flag,\n        _include_agents_list=include_agents_list,\n        save_manifest_path=resolved_manifest,\n        target_input=target_input,\n        dry_run=dry_run,\n        debug=debug,\n        max_turns=max_turns,\n        model=model,\n        stall_timeout=stall_timeout,\n        skip_review=skip_review,\n    )\n    return config\n\n\ndef validate_portify_config(config: PortifyConfig) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Validate a PortifyConfig and return a list of error strings.\n\n    Returns an empty list on success. Never raises — all errors are\n    collected and returned as human-readable strings.\n\n    Checks (in order):\n      1. workflow_path exists\n      2. SKILL.md present at resolved workflow path\n      3. output_dir parent is writable (or can be created)\n      4. CLI name does not collide with existing non-portified modules\n    \"\"\"\n    errors: list[str] = []\n\n    # --- 1. Workflow path existence ---\n    try:\n        resolved = config.resolve_workflow_path()\n    except Exception as exc:\n        errors.append(f\"Cannot resolve workflow path: {exc}\")\n        return errors\n\n    if not resolved.exists():\n        errors.append(\n            f\"Workflow path does not exist: {resolved}\"\n        )\n        return errors  # No point continuing if path missing\n\n    # --- 2. SKILL.md check ---\n    skill_md = resolved / \"SKILL.md\"\n    if not skill_md.exists():\n        errors.append(f\"SKILL.md not found at {resolved}\")\n\n    # --- 3. Output directory writability ---\n    if config.output_dir is not None:\n        out_parent = config.output_dir.parent\n        out_path = config.output_dir\n\n        # Allow path that doesn't exist yet if parent exists and is writable\n        if out_path.exists() and not os.access(out_path, os.W_OK):\n            errors.append(f\"Output path is not writable: {out_path}\")\n        elif not out_path.exists():\n            # Check if parent directory is reachable\n            try:\n                out_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n            except (PermissionError, OSError) as exc:\n                errors.append(f\"Cannot create output directory {out_path}: {exc}\")\n\n    # --- 4. Collision detection ---\n    try:\n        derived_name = config.derive_cli_name()\n        collision_errors = _check_collision(derived_name, config)\n        errors.extend(collision_errors)\n    except Exception as exc:\n        # DerivationFailedError or similar\n        errors.append(str(exc))\n\n    return errors\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# T02.01: Workflow Path Resolution\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef resolve_workflow_path(\n    name_or_path: Union[str, Path],\n    skills_root: Optional[Path] = None,\n) -> Path:\n    \"\"\"Resolve a skill name or path to a concrete skill directory.\n\n    Rules:\n      1. If the input is an existing absolute or relative path to a directory\n         containing SKILL.md → return it directly.\n      2. If the input is a SKILL.md file → return its parent.\n      3. If skills_root is provided, search for matching subdirectories.\n         - Exact match → return it.\n         - Partial match (multiple) → raise AmbiguousPathError.\n         - No match → raise InvalidPathError.\n      4. If no skills_root, treat name_or_path as a literal path.\n\n    Raises:\n        InvalidPathError: Path found but no SKILL.md present.\n        AmbiguousPathError: Partial name matches multiple skill directories.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .models import AmbiguousPathError, InvalidPathError\n\n    p = Path(name_or_path).resolve()\n\n    # Direct path resolution\n    if p.exists():\n        if p.is_file() and p.name == \"SKILL.md\":\n            return p.parent\n        if p.is_dir():\n            if not (p / \"SKILL.md\").exists():\n                raise InvalidPathError(str(p))\n            return p\n        raise InvalidPathError(str(p))\n\n    # Search under skills_root\n    if skills_root and skills_root.exists():\n        name = Path(name_or_path).name\n        candidates = [\n            d for d in skills_root.iterdir()\n            if d.is_dir() and name.lower() in d.name.lower()\n        ]\n        if len(candidates) == 1:\n            candidate = candidates[0]\n            if not (candidate / \"SKILL.md\").exists():\n                raise InvalidPathError(str(candidate))\n            return candidate\n        elif len(candidates) > 1:\n            from .models import AmbiguousPathError\n            raise AmbiguousPathError(str(name), [str(c) for c in candidates])\n        # No match under skills_root → fall through to not-found\n\n    raise InvalidPathError(str(p))\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# T02.02: CLI Name Derivation\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef derive_cli_name(workflow_path: Path, explicit_name: Optional[str] = None) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive the CLI module name from a workflow path.\n\n    If explicit_name is provided, it is used directly (bypasses derivation).\n    Otherwise, derives from the workflow directory name by:\n      1. Stripping 'sc-' prefix\n      2. Stripping '-protocol' suffix\n      3. Normalizing to kebab-case\n\n    Raises:\n        DerivationFailedError: If derivation yields an empty string and no\n                               explicit_name was provided.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .models import DerivationFailedError\n\n    if explicit_name:\n        return explicit_name\n    return _derive_name_from_path(workflow_path)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# T02.03: Collision Detection\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _check_collision(derived_name: str, config: PortifyConfig) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Check if derived CLI name collides with an existing module.\n\n    Returns list of error strings (empty if no collision).\n\n    A collision is blocked unless the existing module's __init__.py contains\n    portification markers ('Generated by' or 'Portified from').\n    \"\"\"\n    from .utils import is_portified_module\n\n    errors: list[str] = []\n\n    # Check known reserved CLI module names\n    normalized = derived_name.replace(\"-\", \"_\")\n    if derived_name in _KNOWN_CLI_MODULES or normalized in _KNOWN_CLI_MODULES:\n        errors.append(\n            f\"CLI name '{derived_name}' collides with existing module \"\n            f\"'{normalized}' — use --name to choose a different name\"\n        )\n        return errors\n\n    # Check filesystem collision under src/superclaude/cli/\n    cli_root = _find_cli_root(config)\n    if cli_root is None:\n        return errors\n\n    candidate = cli_root / normalized\n    if candidate.exists():\n        init_py = candidate / \"__init__.py\"\n        if init_py.exists() and is_portified_module(init_py):\n            # Overwrite allowed\n            return errors\n        errors.append(\n            f\"CLI name '{derived_name}' collides with existing module at \"\n            f\"{candidate} — use --name to choose a different name, or \"\n            \"add portification marker to __init__.py to allow overwrite\"\n        )\n\n    return errors\n\n\ndef _find_cli_root(config: PortifyConfig) -> Optional[Path]:\n    \"\"\"Find the src/superclaude/cli/ root for collision checking.\"\"\"\n    #    placeholder_count: int = 0\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Phase 3 T03.01: TurnLedger (executor-internal budget tracker, OQ-001)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass TurnLedger:\n    \"\"\"Tracks turn budget for Claude-assisted steps.\n\n    One \"turn\" = one Claude subprocess invocation (including retries).\n    can_launch() must return False when remaining turns are insufficient,\n    triggering a HALTED outcome (FR-040).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, total_budget: int) -> None:\n        self._total_budget: int = total_budget\n        self._consumed: int = 0\n\n    @property\n    def total_budget(self) -> int:\n        return self._total_budget\n\n    @property\n    def consumed(self) -> int:\n        return self._consumed\n\n    @property\n    def remaining(self) -> int:\n        return max(0, self._total_budget - self._consumed)\n\n    def consume(self, turns: int = 1) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Record that `turns` turns were consumed.\"\"\"\n        self._consumed += turns\n\n    def can_launch(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return True if at least one turn remains.\"\"\"\n        return self.remaining > 0\n\n    def reset(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Reset consumed turns (for testing only).\"\"\"\n        self._consumed = 0\n\n\ndef _derive_name_from_path(workflow_path: Path) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive CLI module name from a workflow directory path.\n\n    Algorithm:\n      1. Take directory name (not full path).\n      2. Strip leading 'sc-' prefix (case-insensitive).\n      3. Strip trailing '-protocol' suffix (case-insensitive).\n      4. Normalize to lowercase kebab-case.\n      5. If result is empty, raise DerivationFailedError.\n    \"\"\"\n    name = workflow_path.name if workflow_path.is_file() else workflow_path.name\n    # Normalize to lowercase\n    name = name.lower()\n    # Strip sc- prefix\n    if name.startswith(\"sc-\"):\n        name = name[3:]\n    # Strip -protocol suffix\n    if name.endswith(\"-protocol\"):\n        name = name[: -len(\"-protocol\")]\n    # Normalize: keep only lowercase letters, digits, hyphens\n    import re\n    name = re.sub(r\"[^a-z0-9-]\", \"-\", name)\n    # Collapse multiple hyphens\n    name = re.sub(r\"-+\", \"-\", name).strip(\"-\")\n\n    if not name:\n        raise DerivationFailedError(workflow_path.name)\n    return name\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":557,"oldLines":6,"newStart":557,"newLines":8,"lines":["     model: str = \"\"","     stall_timeout: int = 300","     skip_review: bool = False","+    max_convergence: int = 3","+    iteration_timeout: int = 300"," ","     @property","     def results_dir(self) -> Optional[Path]:"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

If omitted, derived\n                  automatically from workflow directory name.\n        commands_dir: Override for commands discovery directory.\n        skills_dir: Override for skills discovery directory.\n        agents_dir: Override for agents discovery directory.\n        include_agents: If a list, enables agent inclusion and stores the list.\n                        If True/False, sets include_agents flag only.\n        save_manifest_path: Path to write the component manifest.\n        dry_run: Limit run to config validation only.\n        debug: Enable debug logging.\n        max_turns: Maximum subprocess turns allowed.\n        model: Claude model identifier.\n        stall_timeout: Seconds before stall detection triggers.\n    \"\"\"\n    wp = Path(workflow_path).resolve()\n    target_input = str(workflow_path)\n\n    # Never raises — all errors are\n    collected and returned as human-readable strings.\n\n    Checks (in order):\n      1. workflow_path exists\n      2. SKILL.md present at resolved workflow path\n      3. output_dir parent is writable (or can be created)\n      4. CLI name does not collide with existing non-portified modules\n    \"\"\"\n    errors: list[str] = []\n\n    # --- 1. Workflow path existence ---\n    try:\n        resolved = config.resolve_workflow_path()\n    except Exception as exc:\n        errors.append(f\"Cannot resolve workflow path: {exc}\")\n        return errors\n\n    if not resolved.exists():\n        errors.append(\n            f\"Workflow path does not exist: {resolved}\"\n        )\n        return errors  # No point continuing if path missing\n\n    # --- 2. SKILL.md check ---\n    skill_md = resolved / \"SKILL.md\"\n    if not skill_md.exists():\n        errors.append(f\"SKILL.md not found at {resolved}\")\n\n    # --- 3. Output directory writability ---\n    if config.output_dir is not None:\n        out_parent = config.output_dir.parent\n        out_path = config.output_dir\n\n        # Allow path that doesn't exist yet if parent exists and is writable\n        if out_path.exists() and not os.access(out_path, os.W_OK):\n            errors.append(f\"Output path is not writable: {out_path}\")\n        elif not out_path.exists():\n            # Check if parent directory is reachable\n            try:\n                out_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n            except (PermissionError, OSError) as exc:\n                errors.append(f\"Cannot create output directory {out_path}: {exc}\")\n\n    # --- 4. Collision detection ---\n    try:\n        derived_name = config.derive_cli_name()\n        collision_errors = _check_collision(derived_name, config)\n        errors.extend(collision_errors)\n    except Exception as exc:\n        # DerivationFailedError or similar\n        errors.append(str(exc))\n\n    return errors\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# T02.01: Workflow Path Resolution\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef resolve_workflow_path(\n    name_or_path: Union[str, Path],\n    skills_root: Optional[Path] = None,\n) -> Path:\n    \"\"\"Resolve a skill name or path to a concrete skill directory.\n\n    Rules:\n      1. If the input is an existing absolute or relative path to a directory\n         containing SKILL.md → return it directly.\n      2. If the input is a SKILL.md file → return its parent.\n      3. If skills_root is provided, search for matching subdirectories.\n         - Exact match → return it.\n         - Partial match (multiple) → raise AmbiguousPathError.\n         - No match → raise InvalidPathError.\n      4. If no skills_root, treat name_or_path as a literal path.\n\n    Raises:\n        InvalidPathError: Path found but no SKILL.md present.\n        AmbiguousPathError: Partial name matches multiple skill directories.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .models import AmbiguousPathError, InvalidPathError\n\n    p = Path(name_or_path).resolve()\n\n    # Direct path resolution\n    if p.exists():\n        if p.is_file() and p.name == \"SKILL.md\":\n            return p.parent\n        if p.is_dir():\n            if not (p / \"SKILL.md\").exists():\n                raise InvalidPathError(str(p))\n            return p\n        raise InvalidPathError(str(p))\n\n    # Search under skills_root\n    if skills_root and skills_root.exists():\n        name = Path(name_or_path).name\n        candidates = [\n            d for d in skills_root.iterdir()\n            if d.is_dir() and name.lower() in d.name.lower()\n        ]\n        if len(candidates) == 1:\n            candidate = candidates[0]\n            if not (candidate / \"SKILL.md\").exists():\n                raise InvalidPathError(str(candidate))\n            return candidate\n        elif len(candidates) > 1:\n            from .models import AmbiguousPathError\n            raise AmbiguousPathError(str(name), [str(c) for c in candidates])\n        # No match under skills_root → fall through to not-found\n\n    raise InvalidPathError(str(p))\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# T02.02: CLI Name Derivation\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef derive_cli_name(workflow_path: Path, explicit_name: Optional[str] = None) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive the CLI module name from a workflow path.\n\n    If explicit_name is provided, it is used directly (bypasses derivation).\n    Otherwise, derives from the workflow directory name by:\n      1. Stripping 'sc-' prefix\n      2. Stripping '-protocol' suffix\n      3. Normalizing to kebab-case\n\n    Raises:\n        DerivationFailedError: If derivation yields an empty string and no\n                               explicit_name was provided.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .models import DerivationFailedError\n\n    if explicit_name:\n        return explicit_name\n    return _derive_name_from_path(workflow_path)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# T02.03: Collision Detection\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _check_collision(derived_name: str, config: PortifyConfig) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Check if derived CLI name collides with an existing module.\n\n    Returns list of error strings (empty if no collision).\n\n    A collision is blocked unless the existing module's __init__.py contains\n    portification markers ('Generated by' or 'Portified from').\n    \"\"\"\n    from .utils import is_portified_module\n\n    errors: list[str] = []\n\n    # Check known reserved CLI module names\n    normalized = derived_name.replace(\"-\", \"_\")\n    if derived_name in _KNOWN_CLI_MODULES or normalized in _KNOWN_CLI_MODULES:\n        errors.append(\n            f\"CLI name '{derived_name}' collides with existing module \"\n            f\"'{normalized}' — use --name to choose a different name\"\n        )\n        return errors\n\n    # Check filesystem collision under src/superclaude/cli/\n    cli_root = _find_cli_root(config)\n    if cli_root is None:\n        return errors\n\n    candidate = cli_root / normalized\n    if candidate.exists():\n        init_py = candidate / \"__init__.py\"\n        if init_py.exists() and is_portified_module(init_py):\n            # Overwrite allowed\n            return errors\n        errors.append(\n            f\"CLI name '{derived_name}' collides with existing module at \"\n            f\"{candidate} — use --name to choose a different name, or \"\n            \"add portification marker to __init__.py to allow overwrite\"\n        )\n\n    return errors\n\n\ndef _find_cli_root(config: PortifyConfig) -> Optional[Path]:\n    \"\"\"Find the src/superclaude/cli/ root for collision checking.\"\"\"\n    # Walk up from workflow_path to find the cli directory\n    search = config.workflow_path\n    for _ in range(10):\n        candidate = search / \"src\" / \"superclaude\" / \"cli\"\n        if candidate.exists():\n            return candidate\n        parent = search.parent\n        if parent == search:\n            break\n        search = parent\n    # Fallback: look relative to the package\n    import superclaude.cli\n    import importlib\n    try:\n        spec = importlib.util.find_spec(\"superclaude.cli\")\n        if spec and spec.submodule_search_locations:\n            return Path(list(spec.submodule_search_locations)[0])\n    except Exception:\n        pass\n    return None\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":116,"oldLines":6,"newStart":116,"newLines":8,"lines":["         model=model,","         stall_timeout=stall_timeout,","         skip_review=skip_review,","+        max_convergence=max_convergence,","+        iteration_timeout=iteration_timeout,","     )","     return config"," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

workflow_path=workflow_path,\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        timeout_seconds=timeout_s,\n    )\n    proc_result = process.run()\n\n    # --- Handle timeout not outer retry) ---\n    engine = ConvergenceEngine(max_iterations=3)\n    engine.submit(IterationResult(\n        iteration=1,\n        unaddressed_criticals=unaddressed,\n        quality_scores=quality_scores,\n    ))\n    convergence_result = engine.result()\n\n    # --- Determine downstream_ready ---\n    downstream_ready = check_downstream_readiness(overall)\n\n    # ---   phase=phase,\n                portify_status=PortifyStatus.FAIL,\n                failure_classification=FailureClassification.USER_REJECTION,\n                gate_tier=gate_tier,\n                artifact_path=str(output_artifact),\n                error_message=\"User rejected panel review artifact\",\n                review_accepted=False,\n            )\n\n    return PortifyStepResult(\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01W7osznyEykxEH9FezCwH6Z","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/cli_portify/steps/panel_review.py has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"07043970-a805-4a56-9ab7-4890b9fec627","uuid":"f15fc424-4cfb-49c0-bba5-d73e763fa19d","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/cli_portify/steps/panel_review.py","oldString":"    return PortifyStepResult(\n        step_name=step_name,\n        step_number=step_number,\n        phase=phase,\n        portify_status=PortifyStatus.PASS,\n        gate_tier=gate_tier,\n        artifact_path=str(output_artifact),\n        iteration_timeout=timeout_s,\n    )","newString":"    # Write panel-review.md as canonical artifact name (alongside panel-reviewed-spec.md)\n    panel_review_path = results_dir / \"panel-review.md\"\n    if output_artifact.exists() and not panel_review_path.exists():\n        import shutil as _shutil\n        _shutil.copy2(str(output_artifact), str(panel_review_path))\n\n    return PortifyStepResult(\n        step_name=step_name,\n        step_number=step_number,\n        phase=phase,\n        portify_status=PortifyStatus.PASS,\n        gate_tier=gate_tier,\n        artifact_path=str(output_artifact),\n        iteration_timeout=timeout_s,\n        review_accepted=True,\n    )","originalFile":"\"\"\"Panel review step (Step 7) for the CLI Portify pipeline.\n\nImplements:\n- run_panel_review(): orchestrate the full panel-review step\n- parse_quality_scores(): extract 4-dimension scores from Claude output\n- compute_overall_score(): mean of 4 quality dimensions\n- check_downstream_readiness(): overall >= 7.0 gate (FR-034, SC-009)\n- count_unaddressed_criticals(): count unresolved CRITICAL findings (FR-032)\n- capture_section_hashes(): hash all ## sections for additive-only enforcement\n- generate_panel_report(): write panel-report.md with YAML frontmatter (FR-035)\n- prompt_user_review(): ask user to accept/reject the panel report\n- DOWNSTREAM_READINESS_THRESHOLD: 7.0 constant\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nfrom superclaude.cli.cli_portify.convergence import (\n    ConvergenceEngine,\n    ConvergenceResult,\n    ConvergenceState,\n    EscalationReason,\n    IterationResult,\n)\nfrom superclaude.cli.cli_portify.models import (\n    FailureClassification,\n    PortifyConfig,\n    PortifyStatus,\n    PortifyStepResult,\n)\nfrom superclaude.cli.cli_portify.process import PortifyProcess\nfrom superclaude.cli.cli_portify.utils import extract_sections, hash_section\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Constants\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nDOWNSTREAM_READINESS_THRESHOLD: float = 7.0\n\n_QUALITY_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (\n    \"clarity\",\n    \"completeness\",\n    \"testability\",\n    \"consistency\",\n)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Quality scoring\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef parse_quality_scores(content: str) -> dict[str, float]:\n    \"\"\"Extract quality scores from Claude output.\n\n    Supports two formats:\n      - Colon format:  ``clarity: 8.5``\n      - Table format:  ``| clarity | 8.5 |``\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict of {dimension: score}. Empty dict if no scores found.\n    \"\"\"\n    scores: dict[str, float] = {}\n\n    # Colon format: \"dimension: 8.5\"\n    colon_pattern = re.compile(\n        r\"^\\s*(\" + \"|\".join(_QUALITY_DIMENSIONS) + r\")\\s*:\\s*(\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)?)\\s*$\",\n        re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,\n    )\n    for m in colon_pattern.finditer(content):\n        dim = m.group(1).lower()\n        scores[dim] = float(m.group(2))\n\n    # Table format: \"| clarity | 8.5 |\"\n    if not scores:\n        table_pattern = re.compile(\n            r\"\\|\\s*(\" + \"|\".join(_QUALITY_DIMENSIONS) + r\")\\s*\\|\\s*(\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)?)\\s*\\|\",\n            re.IGNORECASE,\n        )\n        for m in table_pattern.finditer(content):\n            dim = m.group(1).lower()\n            scores[dim] = float(m.group(2))\n\n    return scores\n\n\ndef compute_overall_score(scores: dict[str, float]) -> float:\n    \"\"\"Return the mean of the 4 quality dimensions (missing dimensions = 0).\"\"\"\n    if not scores:\n        return 0.0\n    total = sum(scores.get(d, 0.0) for d in _QUALITY_DIMENSIONS)\n    return total / 4.0\n\n\ndef check_downstream_readiness(overall: float) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Return True if overall score meets the downstream readiness threshold (FR-034).\"\"\"\n    return overall >= DOWNSTREAM_READINESS_THRESHOLD\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Critical counting\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef count_unaddressed_criticals(content: str) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Count CRITICAL findings NOT marked [RESOLVED] or [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED].\n\n    A CRITICAL is unaddressed when the line contains 'CRITICAL' but does NOT\n    start with a resolved/dismissed marker.\n    \"\"\"\n    count = 0\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.strip()\n        if \"CRITICAL\" not in stripped.upper():\n            continue\n        upper = stripped.upper()\n        # Skip if any resolved marker appears anywhere before CRITICAL\n        critical_pos = upper.find(\"CRITICAL\")\n        prefix = upper[:critical_pos]\n        if \"[RESOLVED]\" in prefix or \"[INCORPORATED]\" in prefix or \"[DISMISSED]\" in prefix:\n            continue\n        count += 1\n    return count\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Section hashing\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef capture_section_hashes(content: str) -> dict[str, str]:\n    \"\"\"Capture hashes of all ## sections in content.\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict of {section_title: sha256_hex}.\n    \"\"\"\n    sections = extract_sections(content)\n    return {title: hash_section(body) for title, body in sections.items()}\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Panel report generation\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef generate_panel_report(\n    convergence_result: ConvergenceResult,\n    quality_scores: dict[str, float],\n    overall_score: float,\n    downstream_ready: bool,\n    output_path: Path,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write panel-report.md with YAML frontmatter and human-readable sections.\n\n    The frontmatter includes all machine-readable fields required by FR-035 and SC-010:\n      terminal_state, iteration_count, overall, downstream_ready,\n      individual quality dimensions, and escalation_reason (if escalated).\n\n    Args:\n        convergence_result: Final convergence engine result.\n        quality_scores: Dict of dimension scores.\n        overall_score: Pre-computed overall (mean of 4 dimensions).\n        downstream_ready: Whether the overall threshold was met.\n        output_path: Where to write panel-report.md.\n    \"\"\"\n    terminal_state = convergence_result.state.value.lower()\n    iteration_count = convergence_result.iterations_completed\n\n    # --- YAML frontmatter ---\n    fm_lines = [\n        \"---\",\n        f\"terminal_state: {terminal_state}\",\n        f\"iteration_count: {iteration_count}\",\n        f\"overall: {overall_score}\",\n        f\"downstream_ready: {'true' if downstream_ready else 'false'}\",\n    ]\n\n    for dim in _QUALITY_DIMENSIONS:\n        if dim in quality_scores:\n            fm_lines.append(f\"{dim}: {quality_scores[dim]}\")\n\n    if convergence_result.escalation_reason is not None:\n        fm_lines.append(f\"escalation_reason: {convergence_result.escalation_reason.value}\")\n\n    fm_lines.append(\"---\")\n    fm_lines.append(\"\")\n\n    # --- Human-readable body ---\n    body_lines: list[str] = []\n\n    body_lines += [\n        \"## Convergence Summary\",\n        \"\",\n        f\"**Terminal State**: {terminal_state.upper()}\",\n        f\"**Iterations Completed**: {iteration_count}\",\n    ]\n\n    if convergence_result.escalation_reason is not None:\n        body_lines.append(f\"**Escalation Reason**: {convergence_result.escalation_reason.value}\")\n\n    body_lines += [\"\", \"## Quality Scores\", \"\"]\n    body_lines.append(\"| Dimension | Score |\")\n    body_lines.append(\"|-----------|-------|\")\n    for dim in _QUALITY_DIMENSIONS:\n        score = quality_scores.get(dim, 0.0)\n        body_lines.append(f\"| {dim} | {score} |\")\n    body_lines.append(f\"| **overall** | {overall_score} |\")\n\n    body_lines += [\n        \"\",\n        \"## Downstream Readiness\",\n        \"\",\n        f\"**downstream_ready**: {'true' if downstream_ready else 'false'}\",\n        \"\",\n        f\"Threshold: {DOWNSTREAM_READINESS_THRESHOLD} — \"\n        + (\"PASSED\" if downstream_ready else \"NOT MET\"),\n        \"\",\n    ]\n\n    content = \"\\n\".join(fm_lines + body_lines)\n    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    output_path.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# User review prompt\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef prompt_user_review(artifact_path: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Ask the user whether to accept the panel report artifact.\n\n    Returns True if accepted, False if rejected/EOF/interrupt.\n    \"\"\"\n    print(f\"\\n[Panel Review] Artifact produced: {artifact_path}\")\n    print(\"Accept and continue? [y/N] \", end=\"\", flush=True)\n    try:\n        response = input().strip().lower()\n    except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):\n        return False\n    return response in (\"y\", \"yes\")\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# run_panel_review\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef run_panel_review(config: PortifyConfig) -> PortifyStepResult:\n    \"\"\"Execute the full panel-review step (Step 7, Phase 4).\n\n    Requires:\n        - ``config.results_dir / \"synthesized-spec.md\"`` (prior step artifact)\n        - ``config.results_dir / \"brainstorm-gaps.md\"``  (prior step artifact)\n\n    Produces:\n        - ``config.results_dir / \"panel-reviewed-spec.md\"``\n        - ``config.results_dir / \"panel-report.md\"``\n\n    Returns:\n        PortifyStepResult with step_name=\"panel-review\", step_number=7, phase=4,\n        gate_tier=\"STRICT\".\n    \"\"\"\n    from superclaude.cli.cli_portify.executor import STEP_REGISTRY\n\n    step_name = \"panel-review\"\n    step_number = 7\n    phase = 4\n    gate_tier = \"STRICT\"\n\n    results_dir = config.results_dir\n    if results_dir is None:\n        return PortifyStepResult(\n            step_name=step_name,\n            step_number=step_number,\n            phase=phase,\n            portify_status=PortifyStatus.FAIL,\n            failure_classification=FailureClassification.MISSING_ARTIFACT,\n            gate_tier=gate_tier,\n            error_message=\"No results_dir configured on PortifyConfig\",\n        )\n\n    # --- Check required prior artifacts ---\n    spec_path = results_dir / \"synthesized-spec.md\"\n    gaps_path = results_dir / \"brainstorm-gaps.md\"\n\n    if not spec_path.exists():\n        return PortifyStepResult(\n            step_name=step_name,\n            step_number=step_number,\n            phase=phase,\n            portify_status=PortifyStatus.FAIL,\n            failure_classification=FailureClassification.MISSING_ARTIFACT,\n            gate_tier=gate_tier,\n            error_message=f\"Required artifact missing: {spec_path}\",\n        )\n\n    if not gaps_path.exists():\n        return PortifyStepResult(\n            step_name=step_name,\n            step_number=step_number,\n            phase=phase,\n            portify_status=PortifyStatus.FAIL,\n            failure_classification=FailureClassification.MISSING_ARTIFACT,\n            gate_tier=gate_tier,\n            error_message=f\"Required artifact missing: {gaps_path}\",\n        )\n\n    # --- Build output paths ---\n    results_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    output_artifact = results_dir / \"panel-reviewed-spec.md\"\n    error_file = results_dir / \"panel-review.err\"\n    report_path = results_dir / \"panel-report.md\"\n\n    # --- Determine timeout from registry ---\n    timeout_s: int = STEP_REGISTRY.get(step_name, {}).get(\"timeout_s\", 1200)\n\n    # --- Build prompt ---\n    spec_content = spec_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    gaps_content = gaps_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    prompt = _build_panel_review_prompt(spec_content, gaps_content)\n\n    # --- Determine working dirs ---\n    work_dir = config.workdir_path or results_dir\n    workflow_path = config.workflow_path\n\n    # --- Execute subprocess ---\n    process = PortifyProcess(\n        prompt=prompt,\n        output_file=output_artifact,\n        error_file=error_file,\n        work_dir=work_dir,\n        workflow_path=workflow_path,\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        timeout_seconds=timeout_s,\n    )\n    proc_result = process.run()\n\n    # --- Handle timeout ---\n    if proc_result.timed_out or proc_result.exit_code == 124:\n        return PortifyStepResult(\n            step_name=step_name,\n            step_number=step_number,\n            phase=phase,\n            portify_status=PortifyStatus.TIMEOUT,\n            failure_classification=FailureClassification.TIMEOUT,\n            gate_tier=gate_tier,\n            error_message=\"panel-review step timed out\",\n            iteration_timeout=timeout_s,\n        )\n\n    # --- Parse output ---\n    output_text = proc_result.stdout_text or \"\"\n    quality_scores = parse_quality_scores(output_text)\n    overall = compute_overall_score(quality_scores)\n    unaddressed = count_unaddressed_criticals(output_text)\n\n    # --- Run convergence engine (internal loop, not outer retry) ---\n    engine = ConvergenceEngine(max_iterations=3)\n    engine.submit(IterationResult(\n        iteration=1,\n        unaddressed_criticals=unaddressed,\n        quality_scores=quality_scores,\n    ))\n    convergence_result = engine.result()\n\n    # --- Determine downstream_ready ---\n    downstream_ready = check_downstream_readiness(overall)\n\n    # --- Emit panel-report.md on BOTH terminal paths ---\n    generate_panel_report(\n        convergence_result=convergence_result,\n        quality_scores=quality_scores,\n        overall_score=overall,\n        downstream_ready=downstream_ready,\n        output_path=report_path,\n    )\n\n    # --- User review gate (if not skipped) ---\n    if not config.skip_review:\n        accepted = prompt_user_review(str(output_artifact))\n        if not accepted:\n            return PortifyStepResult(\n                step_name=step_name,\n                step_number=step_number,\n                phase=phase,\n                portify_status=PortifyStatus.FAIL,\n                failure_classification=FailureClassification.USER_REJECTION,\n                gate_tier=gate_tier,\n                artifact_path=str(output_artifact),\n                error_message=\"User rejected panel review artifact\",\n                review_accepted=False,\n            )\n\n    return PortifyStepResult(\n        step_name=step_name,\n        step_number=step_number,\n        phase=phase,\n        portify_status=PortifyStatus.PASS,\n        gate_tier=gate_tier,\n        artifact_path=str(output_artifact),\n        iteration_timeout=timeout_s,\n    )\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Internal helpers\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _build_panel_review_prompt(spec_content: str, gaps_content: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build the panel-review prompt for Claude.\"\"\"\n    return f\"\"\"You are conducting a final quality review of a CLI pipeline specification.\n\n## Synthesized Specification\n\n{spec_content}\n\n## Gap Analysis Findings\n\n{gaps_content}\n\n## Review Instructions\n\nReview the specification against the gap analysis findings. For each finding:\n1. Assess whether it has been addressed in the specification.\n2. Rate the severity: CRITICAL (must fix), MAJOR (should fix), MINOR (nice to fix).\n3.
